notes: Single (modelId, providerId) primary pair pinned for every stage of the agentic loop. Resilience is OpenRouter-side: at runtime itotori sends provider.order=[providerId] + provider.allow_fallbacks=true + provider.zdr=true + provider.data_collection=deny, and OpenRouter routes within the account ZDR allow-list on an HTTP 429, recording whichever provider actually served (ITOTORI-241; UTSUSHI-231 live run completed when OR served DigitalOcean on a Fireworks miss). The superseded app-level alternateProviders[] + failoverPredicate failover chain (ITOTORI-238/239/240) was REMOVED — it was redundant with OR-side fallback and could double-handle a 429 OR had already resolved. If every ZDR-allow-list provider is at quota, OpenRouter returns the terminal error and the run surfaces it verbatim as a ModelProviderError; there is no app-level retry layered on top. The enUsSentinel substring is woven into the translation prompt so the patched Seen.txt's replay produces a TextLine whose bodyUtf8 contains the substring verbatim. ASCII-only by deliberate choice: the leading SJIS bracket required by the KAIFUU-191 lexer is wrapped around the sentinel by the patch step, not by the prompt. Per-stage zdr / fallbackModels / seed / maxPriceUsd are intentionally omitted: the parser fills in canonical defaults. Operator-level zdr downgrades require OPENROUTER_ZDR_DOWNGRADE; no file-level escape hatch.
